# MCP Tutorial Content Generation - Implementation Plan

## Context

**Original Request**: Create initial content for an MCP (Model Context Protocol) interactive tutorial website. The content should be generated by AI based on MCP documentation, covering 6 modules with clear explanations, executable code examples, inline quizzes, and section-end assessments.

**Decision**: Generate draft content using AI agents that will be reviewed and edited by the user.

**Codebase State**: Fresh project with only specification document at `.omc/autopilot/spec.md`. No code or content exists yet.

**Technical Stack** (from spec):
- Next.js 15 + React 19
- MDX for content authoring
- Shiki for code highlighting
- Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui for UI components
- LocalStorage for progress persistence

---

## Work Objectives

### Core Objective
Generate comprehensive, educational MDX content for all 6 modules of the MCP tutorial, including code examples, quizzes, and interactive elements.

### Deliverables
1. **6 Module MDX Files** with frontmatter, content, quizzes
2. **Code Examples** - TypeScript/Node.js MCP server implementations
3. **Quiz Content** - 2-3 inline quizzes per section, 3-5 questions per section-end
4. **Supporting Assets** - Diagram descriptions (ASCII/markdown), config files
5. **Content Manifest** - TypeScript file defining tutorial structure

### Definition of Done
- All 6 modules have complete MDX content
- Each module has estimated completion time
- All code examples are syntactically valid TypeScript/Node.js
- Inline quizzes distributed throughout sections (2-3 per section)
- Section-end quizzes cover key concepts (3-5 questions each)
- Content follows the structure defined in spec.md
- All content saved to `src/content/tutorials/`

---

## Must Have / Must NOT Have

### Must Have
✅ Prerequisites module - Node.js, TypeScript, Docker verification
✅ MCP Concepts module - Architecture, core concepts (Resources, Tools, Prompts, Transports)
✅ First Server module - Step-by-step hello-world MCP server in TypeScript
✅ Tools & Resources module - Calculator tool, file reading resource examples
✅ Prompts module - Template prompts for LLM interactions
✅ Deployment module - Local, Docker, and cloud/VPS deployment
✅ Copy-paste executable code examples
✅ Inline quizzes (2-3 per section)
✅ Section-end quizzes (3-5 questions each)
✅ Estimated completion times per module

### Must NOT Have
❌ Video content (text/code only)
❌ Full code playground implementation (future enhancement)
❌ User authentication flows
❌ Multi-language support (English only)
❌ MCP Inspector embedded integration (reference only)

---

## Task Flow and Dependencies

### Phase 1: Research & Documentation Gathering
**Can parallelize**: All research tasks
```
1.1 Research MCP official docs → Module 2, 3, 4, 5
1.2 Research TypeScript MCP SDK → Module 3, 4, 5
1.3 Research deployment best practices → Module 6
└── Outputs: Research summaries in .omc/research/
```

### Phase 2: Content Structure Setup
**Sequential**
```
2.1 Create directory structure
2.2 Generate content manifest (tutorial-structure.ts)
2.3 Create MDX component definitions
└── Output: Ready content framework
```

### Phase 3: Content Generation (Per Module)
**Can parallelize**: All modules after Phase 2
```
Module 1: Prerequisites (30 min)
├── 3.1.1 Node.js setup verification
├── 3.1.2 TypeScript setup verification
├── 3.1.3 Docker setup verification
└── 3.1.4 Environment validation script

Module 2: MCP Concepts (45 min)
├── 3.2.1 What is MCP?
├── 3.2.2 Architecture diagram
├── 3.2.3 Core concepts: Resources
├── 3.2.4 Core concepts: Tools
├── 3.2.5 Core concepts: Prompts
├── 3.2.6 Core concepts: Transports
└── 3.2.7 MCP capabilities & negotiation

Module 3: First Server (45 min)
├── 3.3.1 Project initialization
├── 3.3.2 Basic server structure
├── 3.3.3 Adding first tool
├── 3.3.4 Running locally
├── 3.3.5 Testing with MCP Inspector
└── 3.3.6 Debugging basics

Module 4: Tools & Resources (60 min)
├── 3.4.1 Understanding tools
├── 3.4.2 Calculator tool example
├── 3.4.3 Tool error handling
├── 3.4.4 Understanding resources
├── 3.4.5 File reading resource
├── 3.4.6 Resource subscriptions
└── 3.4.7 Best practices

Module 5: Prompts (30 min)
├── 3.5.1 What are prompts?
├── 3.5.2 Defining static prompts
├── 3.5.3 Dynamic prompts with arguments
├── 3.5.4 Prompt templates
└── 3.5.5 Use cases and examples

Module 6: Deployment (60 min)
├── 3.6.1 Local development setup
├── 3.6.2 Environment configuration
├── 3.6.3 Building for production
├── 3.6.4 Docker containerization
├── 3.6.5 Docker Compose setup
├── 3.6.6 VPS deployment (Linux)
├── 3.6.7 Cloud deployment options
└── 3.6.8 Monitoring and logging
```

### Phase 4: Review & Validation
**Sequential**
```
4.1 Content completeness check
4.2 Code example syntax validation
4.3 Quiz answer key validation
4.4 Frontmatter completeness check
└── Output: Validation report
```

#### 2.2 Generate Content Manifest
**Agent**: `writer` (haiku) + `executor` (sonnet)
**Task**: Create TypeScript file defining tutorial structure

**Expected Output**: `src/lib/navigation/tutorial-structure.ts`
```typescript
export interface TutorialModule {
  id: string;
  title: string;
  description: string;
  estimatedTime: number; // in minutes
  order: number;
  sections: TutorialSection[];
}

export interface TutorialSection {
  id: string;
  slug: string;
  title: string;
  order: number;
  contentPath: string;
}

export const TUTORIAL_STRUCTURE: TutorialModule[] = [
  {
    id: 'prerequisites',
    title: 'Prerequisites',
    description: 'Set up your development environment',
    estimatedTime: 30,
    order: 1,
    sections: [
      // ... sections
    ]
  },
  // ... all 6 modules
];
```

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] All 6 modules defined
- [ ] Each module has sections listed
- [ ] Section slugs are URL-safe

```typescript
}

}

  filename?: string;
  language: string;
  code: string;
  showLineNumbers?: boolean;
  highlightLines?: number[];
}

  type: 'info' | 'warning' | 'tip' | 'error' | 'success';
  title?: string;
  content: string;
}
```

```typescript
    command: 'tsc --version',
    required: true

    command: 'docker --version',

];
```

```typescript
// Resource example
server.addResource({
  uri: "config://app",
  name: "Application Config",
  description: "Current application configuration",
  mimeType: "application/json",
  async read() {
    return {
      contents: [{
        uri: "config://app",
        mimeType: "application/json",
        text: JSON.stringify(config, null, 2)
      }]
    };
  }
});
```

- Section-end quiz (5 questions)

**Code Example**:
```typescript
server.addTool({
  name: "calculate",
  description: "Perform a calculation",
  inputSchema: {
    type: "object",
    properties: {
      expression: {
        type: "string",
        description: "Math expression to evaluate"
      }
    },
    required: ["expression"]
  },
  async (args) => {
    const result = eval(args.expression);
    return {
      content: [{
        type: "text",
        text: `Result: ${result}`
      }]
    };
  }
});
```

##### 3.3.2 Section: Basic Server Structure
**Agent**: `writer` (haiku)
**Expected Output**: `src/content/tutorials/first-server/02-server-structure.mdx`

**Content Requirements**:
- Import Server class
- Create server instance
- Server configuration
- stdio transport setup
- Start server
- 3 inline quizzes
- Section-end quiz (5 questions)

**Complete Code Example**:
```typescript
#!/usr/bin/env node

import { Server } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/index.js";
import { StdioServerTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js";
import {
  CallToolRequestSchema,
  ListToolsRequestSchema,
} from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/types.js";

// Create server instance
const server = new Server(
  {
    name: "hello-world-server",
    version: "0.1.0",
  },
  {
    capabilities: {
      tools: {},
    },
  }
);

// List available tools
server.setRequestHandler(ListToolsRequestSchema, async () => {
  return {
    tools: [
      {
        name: "hello",
        description: "Say hello",
        inputSchema: {
          type: "object",
          properties: {
            name: {
              type: "string",
              description: "Name to greet",
            },
          },
          required: ["name"],
        },
      },
    ],
  };
});

// Handle tool execution
server.setRequestHandler(CallToolRequestSchema, async (request) => {
  const { name, arguments: args } = request.params;

  if (name === "hello") {
    const greeting = `Hello, ${args.name}!`;
    return {
      content: [
        {
          type: "text",
          text: greeting,
        },
      ],
    };
  }

  throw new Error(`Unknown tool: ${name}`);
});

// Start server
async function main() {
  const transport = new StdioServerTransport();
  await server.connect(transport);
  console.error("Hello World MCP server running on stdio");
}

main().catch((error) => {
  console.error("Server error:", error);
  process.exit(1);
});
```

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Complete working example
- [ ] Line-by-line explanation
- [ ] 3 inline quizzes
- [ ] 5-question section-end quiz

##### 3.4.2 Section: Calculator Tool Example
**Agent**: `writer` (haiku)
**Expected Output**: `src/content/tutorials/tools-resources/02-calculator-tool.mdx`

**Content Requirements**:
- Complete calculator implementation
- Multiple operations (add, subtract, multiply, divide)
- Error handling (division by zero)
- Type validation
- 3 inline quizzes
- Section-end quiz (5 questions)

**Code Example**: Full calculator tool
```typescript
server.addTool({
  name: "calculate",
  description: "Perform basic arithmetic operations",
  inputSchema: {
    type: "object",
    properties: {
      operation: {
        type: "string",
        enum: ["add", "subtract", "multiply", "divide"],
        description: "Operation to perform"
      },
      a: {
        type: "number",
        description: "First number"
      },
      b: {
        type: "number",
        description: "Second number"
      }
    },
    required: ["operation", "a", "b"]
  }
}, async (args) => {
  const { operation, a, b } = args;

  switch (operation) {
    case "add":
      return { content: [{ type: "text", text: `${a + b}` }] };
    case "subtract":
      return { content: [{ type: "text", text: `${a - b}` }] };
    case "multiply":
      return { content: [{ type: "text", text: `${a * b}` }] };
    case "divide":
      if (b === 0) {
        throw new Error("Division by zero");
      }
      return { content: [{ type: "text", text: `${a / b}` }] };
    default:
      throw new Error(`Unknown operation: ${operation}`);
  }
});
```

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Complete working code
- [ ] All 4 operations
- [ ] Error handling
- [ ] 3 inline quizzes
- [ ] 5-question section-end quiz

##### 3.4.5 Section: File Reading Resource
**Agent**: `writer` (haiku)
**Expected Output**: `src/content/tutorials/tools-resources/05-file-resource.mdx`

**Content Requirements**:
- Complete file reading resource
- Path validation
- Error handling
- Security considerations
- 3 inline quizzes
- Section-end quiz (5 questions)

**Code Example**:
```typescript
import * as fs from 'fs/promises';
import * as path from 'path';

server.addResource({
  uri: "file://read",
  name: "File Reader",
  description: "Read a file from the filesystem",
  mimeType: "text/plain",
}, async (uri) => {
  const filePath = uri.substring(7); // Remove "file://" prefix

  // Security: prevent path traversal
  const normalizedPath = path.normalize(filePath);
  if (normalizedPath !== filePath) {
    throw new Error("Path traversal detected");
  }

  const content = await fs.readFile(normalizedPath, 'utf-8');
  return {
    contents: [{
      uri,
      mimeType: "text/plain",
      text: content
    }]
  };
});
```

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Working code example
- [ ] Security explanation
- [ ] Error handling
- [ ] 3 inline quizzes
- [ ] 5-question section-end quiz

##### 3.5.2 Section: Defining Static Prompts
**Agent**: `writer` (haiku)
**Expected Output**: `src/content/tutorials/prompts/02-static-prompts.mdx`

**Content Requirements**:
- Static prompt registration
- Prompt template
- Message format
- 2 inline quizzes
- Section-end quiz (4 questions)

**Code Example**:
```typescript
server.addPrompt({
  name: "code-review",
  description: "Review code for best practices",
  arguments: [
    {
      name: "language",
      description: "Programming language",
      required: false
    }
  ]
}, async (args) => {
  const language = args?.language || "the code";

  return {
    messages: [
      {
        role: "user",
        content: {
          type: "text",
          text: `Please review the following ${language} code for best practices, potential bugs, and improvements:\n\n{{code}}`
        }
      }
    ]
  };
});
```

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Working example
- [ ] 2 inline quizzes
- [ ] 4-question section-end quiz

##### 3.6.2 Section: Environment Configuration
**Agent**: `writer` (haiku)
**Expected Output**: `src/content/tutorials/deployment/02-environment-config.mdx`

**Content Requirements**:
- Environment variables
- .env files
- Configuration validation
- Security best practices
- 2 inline quizzes
- Section-end quiz (3 questions)

**Code Example**:
```typescript
import { config } from 'dotenv';

config();

const requiredEnvVars = ['API_KEY', 'SERVER_NAME'];

for (const envVar of requiredEnvVars) {
  if (!process.env[envVar]) {
    throw new Error(`Missing required environment variable: ${envVar}`);
  }
}

const serverConfig = {
  name: process.env.SERVER_NAME!,
  apiKey: process.env.API_KEY!,
  port: parseInt(process.env.PORT || '3000')
};
```

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] dotenv usage
- [ ] Validation code
- [ ] Security tips
- [ ] 2 inline quizzes
- [ ] 3-question section-end quiz

##### 3.6.4 Section: Docker Containerization
**Agent**: `writer` (haiku)
**Expected Output**: `src/content/tutorials/deployment/04-docker.mdx`

**Content Requirements**:
- Why Docker
- Dockerfile explanation
- Multi-stage builds
- Image optimization
- 3 inline quizzes
- Section-end quiz (5 questions)

**Complete Dockerfile**:
```dockerfile
# Build stage
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app

COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --only=production

COPY . .
RUN npm run build

# Production stage
FROM node:20-alpine
WORKDIR /app

COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY package.json .

ENV NODE_ENV=production

ENTRYPOINT ["node", "dist/index.js"]
```

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Complete Dockerfile
- [ ] Line-by-line explanation
- [ ] Build/run commands
- [ ] 3 inline quizzes
- [ ] 5-question section-end quiz

##### 3.6.5 Section: Docker Compose Setup
**Agent**: `writer` (haiku)
**Expected Output**: `src/content/tutorials/deployment/05-docker-compose.mdx`

**Content Requirements**:
- docker-compose.yml
- Multi-container setup
- Volume mounting
- Networking
- 2 inline quizzes
- Section-end quiz (4 questions)

**docker-compose.yml**:
```yaml
version: '3.8'

services:
  mcp-server:
    build: .
    container_name: my-mcp-server
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
      - API_KEY=${API_KEY}
    volumes:
      - ./data:/app/data
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
```

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Complete docker-compose.yml
- [ ] Usage commands
- [ ] 2 inline quizzes
- [ ] 4-question section-end quiz

##### 3.6.6 Section: VPS Deployment (Linux)
**Agent**: `writer` (haiku)
**Expected Output**: `src/content/tutorials/deployment/06-vps-deployment.mdx`

**Content Requirements**:
- Choose VPS provider
- Server setup (Ubuntu)
- Docker installation on VPS
- Deploy container
- Reverse proxy (nginx)
- SSL certificates
- 3 inline quizzes
- Section-end quiz (5 questions)

**Commands**:
```bash
# SSH into VPS
ssh user@your-vps-ip

# Update system
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y

# Install Docker
curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com -o get-docker.sh
sudo sh get-docker.sh

# Copy files
scp docker-compose.yml user@your-vps-ip:/app/

# Run on VPS
docker-compose up -d
```

**nginx config**:
```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name your-domain.com;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://localhost:3000;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
    }
}
```

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Step-by-step commands
- [ ] nginx config
- [ ] SSL setup (certbot)
- [ ] 3 inline quizzes
- [ ] 5-question section-end quiz

- 2 inline quizzes

**Health check implementation**:
```typescript
server.addTool({
  name: "health",
  description: "Check server health",
  inputSchema: {
    type: "object",
    properties: {}
  }
}, async () => {
  return {
    content: [{
      type: "text",
      text: JSON.stringify({
        status: "healthy",
        uptime: process.uptime(),
        memory: process.memoryUsage(),
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
      }, null, 2)
    }]
  };
});
```

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Health check code
- [ ] Logging patterns
- [ ] 2 inline quizzes
- [ ] 3-question section-end quiz

### Phase 4: Review & Validation

#### 4.1 Content Completeness Check
**Agent**: `verifier` (sonnet)
**Task**: Verify all content is complete

**Checklist**:
- [ ] All 6 modules exist
- [ ] Each module has correct number of sections
- [ ] All sections have MDX files
- [ ] All frontmatter is present
- [ ] All code blocks have language specified
- [ ] All quizzes have answer keys

**Expected Output**: `.omc/reports/content-completeness.md`

---

#### 4.2 Code Example Syntax Validation
**Agent**: `build-fixer` (sonnet)
**Task**: Validate all TypeScript code examples

**Commands**:
```bash
# Extract all code blocks from MDX
# Validate TypeScript syntax
# Report any errors
```

**Expected Output**: `.omc/reports/code-validation.md`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] All TypeScript examples compile
- [ ] All bash commands are valid
- [ ] All JSON is valid
- [ ] All YAML frontmatter is valid

---

#### 4.3 Quiz Answer Key Validation
**Agent**: `analyst` (sonnet)
**Task**: Validate quiz questions and answers

**Checks**:
- [ ] All questions have correct answer marked
- [ ] All inline quizzes have explanations
- [ ] All section-end quizzes have 3-5 questions
- [ ] CorrectAnswer index is valid (within options range)
- [ ] No duplicate questions in same section

**Expected Output**: `.omc/reports/quiz-validation.md`

---

#### 4.4 Frontmatter Completeness Check
**Agent**: `verifier` (haiku)
**Task**: Verify all frontmatter is complete

**Required Fields**:
```yaml
title: string
module: string (must match folder)
order: number
estimatedTime: number (minutes)
quiz: array (for section-end)
```

**Expected Output**: `.omc/reports/frontmatter-validation.md`

---

## Commit Strategy

### Phase 1: Research
```bash
git add .omc/research/
git commit -m "feat(research): add MCP documentation research

- Research official MCP protocol docs
- Research TypeScript SDK API
- Research deployment best practices

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

### Phase 2: Structure
```bash
git add src/lib/navigation/ src/lib/mdx/ src/components/
git commit -m "feat(structure): add content framework

- Create directory structure
- Add tutorial structure manifest
- Add MDX component type definitions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

### Phase 3: Content (Per Module)
```bash
git add src/content/tutorials/prerequisites/
git commit -m "feat(content): add Prerequisites module

- Node.js setup with verification
- TypeScript setup guide
- Docker installation guide
- Environment validation script

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"

git add src/content/tutorials/mcp-concepts/
git commit -m "feat(content): add MCP Concepts module

- What is MCP introduction
- Architecture deep dive
- Core concepts: Resources, Tools, Prompts, Transports
- Capability negotiation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"

# ... similar for other modules
```

### Phase 4: Validation
```bash
git add .omc/reports/
git commit -m "test(content): add validation reports

- Content completeness check
- Code syntax validation
- Quiz answer key validation
- Frontmatter validation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

---

## Success Criteria

### Content Quality
- [ ] All 6 modules complete with all sections
- [ ] Total inline quizzes: ~40-50
- [ ] Total section-end questions: ~80-100
- [ ] All code examples are syntactically valid
- [ ] All quiz answers are correct
- [ ] All frontmatter is consistent

### Educational Effectiveness
- [ ] Explanations are clear and concise
- [ ] Code examples are copy-paste executable
- [ ] Quizzes reinforce key concepts
- [ ] Progression from basic to advanced
- [ ] Each module builds on previous knowledge

### Technical Completeness
- [ ] MDX files are valid
- [ ] Frontmatter matches required schema
- [ ] Tutorial structure is importable
- [ ] All paths and references are correct

---

## Parallel Execution Opportunities

### Phase 1: Maximum Parallelization (3 tasks)
```
[1.1 Research MCP Docs]     [1.2 Research TypeScript SDK]  [1.3 Research Deployment]
        │                          │                            │
        └──────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────┘
                                    ↓
                           All research complete
```

### Phase 3: Module Generation (6 parallel streams)
```
      ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
      │                                                       │
[3.1 Prerequisites]  [3.2 MCP Concepts]  [3.3 First Server]
      │                       │                     │
      ↓                       ↓                     ↓
  Complete               Complete              Complete

      │                       │                     │

[3.4 Tools & Resources]  [3.5 Prompts]  [3.6 Deployment]
      │                       │                  │
      ↓                       ↓                  ↓
  Complete               Complete           Complete
      │                       │                  │
      └───────────────────────┴──────────────────┘
                    ↓
            All modules complete
```

**Recommended**: After Phase 1 research is complete, spawn 6 `writer` agents in parallel to generate all module content simultaneously.

---

## Agent Routing Summary

| Phase | Task | Agent | Model | Parallel? |
|-------|------|-------|-------|-----------|
| 1.1 | Research MCP docs | `dependency-expert` | sonnet | ✅ Yes |
| 1.2 | Research TypeScript SDK | `dependency-expert` | sonnet | ✅ Yes |
| 1.3 | Research deployment | `dependency-expert` | sonnet | ✅ Yes |
| 2.1 | Create directories | `executor` | sonnet | ❌ No |
| 2.2 | Content manifest | `writer` + `executor` | haiku + sonnet | ❌ No |
| 2.3 | MDX components | `executor` | sonnet | ❌ No |
| 3.1-3.6 | Generate module content | `writer` | haiku | ✅ Yes |
| 4.1 | Completeness check | `verifier` | sonnet | ❌ No |
| 4.2 | Code validation | `build-fixer` | sonnet | ❌ No |
| 4.3 | Quiz validation | `analyst` | sonnet | ❌ No |
| 4.4 | Frontmatter check | `verifier` | haiku | ❌ No |

**Total Estimated Time**: 4-6 hours (with parallelization)

---

## Risk Mitigation

### Risk 1: Research Gaps
**Mitigation**: Cross-reference multiple sources (official docs, GitHub repo, npm package)

### Risk 2: Code Examples Not Working
**Mitigation**: Build and run all examples during validation phase (4.2)

### Risk 3: Inconsistent Quiz Difficulty
**Mitigation**: Review quiz distribution during validation (4.3)

### Risk 4: Missing Content Dependencies
**Mitigation**: Phase 1 research must complete before Phase 3 content generation

---

## Handoff Instructions

After plan confirmation, run:

```bash
/oh-my-claudecode:start-work mcp-tutorial-content-generation
```

This will:
1. Load this implementation plan
2. Spawn agents for each phase
3. Execute tasks in dependency order
4. Validate outputs
5. Generate reports

---

**Plan Created**: 2026-02-22
**Estimated Duration**: 4-6 hours
**Complexity**: MEDIUM (content generation with technical depth)
**Agent Count**: 20+ agents (research, writing, validation)
